clinical_history: What is the underlying rhythm here and what else is going on?  Clue:  Take the fifth, beat, that is.
diagnosis: Atrial fibrillation with a moderate ventricular response plus a single ventricular pacemaker escape spike (fifth beat, which occurs after about a 1 sec pause).  The fifth beat is a so-called pseudo-fusion beat. With pseudo-fusion beats, the pacemaker spike falls within the native QRS, so the QRS morphology is not a true hybrid, as it is with actual pacemaker fusion beats.
